pattern: Each number's digit sequence can be read straight off its base-10 string form (or peeled with repeated mod-10 division, reversed), then flattened in array order.
hints: You don't need any math trick beyond reading each number's digits one at a time, left to right.
Convert each integer to its digit sequence individually, then flatten those sequences into one answer array in the same order the numbers appear.
Either stringify each number and map its characters back to digits, or repeatedly peel off the last digit with mod 10 / divide by 10 and reverse the collected digits before appending.

edge_inputs.description: numbers containing internal and trailing zeros
maximum value at the upper constraint bound
numbers whose digit counts differ, must not pad or truncate
leading nonzero digit followed by zeros must not collapse away the zeros
single element at the max length boundary is not tested here, but a longer mixed array is

stress.description: random-length array of random values across the full magnitude range
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 1000))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
